title: Eva vs Harry
wikitext:
[]: Eva vs. Harry             Quote Post by Djeheuty » Wed Oct 05, 2005 8:52 am [url]http://www.mugglenet.com/editorials/editorials/edit-sjester01.shtml[/url] and they say you can't over analyzing Evangelion

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Wed Oct 05, 2005 11:16 am It was a doomed effort. The author forgot that Evangelion itself is a self confessed amalgamation of a whole plethora of anime and other cliches, themes and stereotypes. Thus one could probably compare Evangelion to Moby Dick or The Cantabury Tales with a reasonable level of sucess.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Oct 05, 2005 1:55 pm OMF, next time someone accuses you of overanalysis - just quote that URL.

[]:             Quote Post by Winslow Leach » Wed Oct 05, 2005 6:26 pm Wow.

[]:             Quote Post by geishaboy » Wed Oct 05, 2005 7:19 pm Well, THAT ones going straight into my bookmarks.

[]:             Quote Post by reagen » Thu Oct 06, 2005 6:16 am Wow!! Pretty big thinking there...

[]:             Quote Post by Punio4 » Thu Oct 06, 2005 7:49 am That's the biggest pile of BS i have seen in a long time...

[]:             Quote Post by Shnooks » Thu Oct 06, 2005 2:08 pm What? They're...almost even completely different time periods, let alone different GENRES.

[]:             Quote Post by drinian » Thu Oct 06, 2005 4:41 pm Shnooks wrote: What? They're...almost even completely different time periods, let alone different GENRES.

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Thu Oct 06, 2005 9:28 pm Someone must rectify this by assulting the associated forum with the light of truth. Not me though. Truth is really not my thing.
